boostingModel.scope: RESOLVED — the narrow reading is correct.
boostingModel.changedIn: 2.2.1 — official changelog: 'Beacon boosting is now additive instead of multiplicative.'
boostingModel.alsoChangedIn2_2_1: 'Slightly increased the strength of unpowered Beacons, and slightly decreased the strength of powered Beacons.'

sources.vibrant: +1 tier (chance-based; Assistant III+; +50% chance with High Spirits mission)
sources.rainbow: guarantees vibrant beacons for a duration

boostingModel.IMPACT: REVISED. The additive change did NOT broadly devalue aqua. The headline aqua-stack combo still reaches White's maximum, so aqua-stacking remains a core tool and the guide's pre-patch priorities are largely still valid. Earlier alarm here was overstated: the changelog's blanket wording implied a sweeping nerf, but the measured outcome matches the guide's narrower reading (aqua and rainbow no longer multiply WITH EACH OTHER).
boostingModel.CONSEQUENCE: Confirmed: vibrant-aqua + White = +30 challenges = White's cap. The separate claim of "aqua-stacked White for 42 challenges" (Lootrun General Tips) is therefore WRONG or stale — 30 is the ceiling.
boostingModel.resolvedBy: user, 2026-07-21: a vibrant-aqua'd White grants 30 challenges, which is White at max power.

measuredPoints.note: White at maximum power. Lower tiers unmeasured.

offerRules.rule: A beacon that is OFFERED at challenge N cannot be offered at challenge N+1. Trigger is APPEARING, not being taken.
offerRules.STRATEGIC_CONSEQUENCE: Narrower than first thought: only red and green can be thinned from the pool. The sharper consequence is defensive — GREEN self-excludes, so a green you decline is GONE next challenge. When time is low and green is offered, it is take-now-or-lose-it, not a rankable preference.
offerRules.ENGINE_REQUIREMENT: State must carry `excludedNext: BeaconColor[]`, populated from the OFFER (all colours shown), not from the taken beacon. Any legality check for challenge N+1 must subtract it.

participatingColors.changedRecently: Aqua and orange USED to self-exclude and no longer do as of a recent patch. The wiki's aqua/red/green list is stale.
participatingColors.notExclusion: darkGrey, white and grey also stop appearing, but via USE CAPS (1/1/3), not exclusion. Different mechanic, modelled separately as maxUses.

offerRules.note: Still load-bearing for the timeout guard: 'aqua offered + timeout risk -> take green' is safe because aqua cannot reappear next challenge anyway.
offerRules.correctionHistory: Modelled twice wrongly: first as a per-colour quirk of aqua/red/green keyed on TAKING; then as a broad set incl. orange keyed on OFFERING. Correct: keyed on OFFERING, set = {red, green}.

beacons.name: Blue Beacon
beacons.effect: Choose a Boon at 100/200/300/400% potency by tier.
beacons.changedIn: 2.2.0 — boosted Blue Beacons now increase Boon strength (previously tier was irrelevant here).
beacons.notes: Tier now matters. Downrank heavily while Ultimate Sacrifice is active, or once Hoarder is online.

beacons.name: Purple Beacon
beacons.effect: Grants Curses and Reward Pulls together.
beacons.notes: Core of curse_stack. Porphyrophobia doubles its pulls and adds a curse ON OFFER, not on take.

beacons.name: Yellow Beacon
beacons.effect: Spawns 2-5 Flying Chests by tier alongside the challenge reward chest.

beacons.name: Aqua Beacon
beacons.notes: RE-EVALUATE. Historically top-2 priority; the 2.2.1 additive change plus powered-beacon nerf undercuts the whole aqua-first meta. Beleza Pura (boosts ALL offered beacons from one aqua) is now comparatively better than holding aqua yourself.
beacons.conflict: Guide Beacon List says "Cannot be offered two challenges in a row!" — CONTRADICTED by user (2026-07-21): aqua can be reoffered as of a recent patch. User wins; the Beacon List tab is stale here, as its Missions tab is. noRepeatAfterOffer stays false.

beacons.name: Orange Beacon
beacons.effect: +1 Beacon Choice for the next 10 challenges (duration scales with tier; the +1 bonus never does).
beacons.notes: Choice count is the input to nearly every other decision — more options strictly dominate. Likely the most robust early pick post-2.2.1, since it is unaffected by the boosting rework.

tiers.durationNote: 10/15/20/25 by tier — user-corrected 2026-07-24, and this matches the guide Beacon List exactly. Supersedes an earlier user figure of 5/10/15/25.

beacons.conflictHistory: Recorded as 5/10/15/25 on 2026-07-22 from a worked example, which overrode the Beacon List. Corrected back on 2026-07-24 when the user restated 10/15/20/25 — now agreeing with the Beacon List, i.e. two independent sources. The structural findings from that earlier example (independent per-orange timers; base beacon choices = 3) came from the CHOICE COUNTS, not the durations, so they still stand.

beacons.name: Green Beacon
beacons.effect: Adds time. Mobs gain no buffs after a green challenge.
constraints: cannot add time above the 15 minute cap
beacons.notes: The timeout guard. Disabled by the Chronotrigger trial for 12 challenges. May reset Thrill Seeker (unconfirmed post-2.2.1 rework).

beacons.name: White Beacon
beacons.effect: Extends challenge count. Max power (vibrant-aqua boosted) = +30 challenges.
beacons.notes: Capped at +30. Hard anti-synergy with Knife Edge. +1 use available via Monochromokopia.

beacons.name: Grey Beacon
beacons.availability: First mission at challenge 4; stops appearing around challenge 30.
beacons.notes: Only ~3 mission slots exist, which is precisely why the first pick effectively commits the archetype. +1 available via Monochromokopia.

beacons.name: Red Beacon
beacons.effect: Adds challenges, but the next challenges grant no time extension.
beacons.notes: 'Red debt'. Feeds Thrill Seeker. Priority rises near run end when challenges, not time, are the binding constraint.

beacons.name: Pink Beacon
beacons.notes: New enough that older guides omit it entirely. Makes reroll_spam (Gourmand + Optimism) a real archetype rather than a curiosity.

beacons.name: Rainbow Beacon
beacons.effect: Guarantees vibrant beacons for a duration.
beacons.changedIn: 2.2.1 — 'Rainbow Beacons only appear after at least 10 challenges were completed, gradually increasing the chance of them appearing until they show up.'
beacons.notes: STRATEGY-RELEVANT: this is a pity timer, not a flat probability. P(rainbow) rises monotonically with challenges since 10 until it fires. Therefore rerolling to 'force rainbow' at challenge 11 is near-worthless (low hazard), while patience is rewarded — the opposite of the flat-probability assumption in older guides. The offer model must implement a real hazard function here.

lastChallengeFailures.note: These do nothing on the final challenge — the advisor must suppress recommending them there.

tierIndexNote: All  arrays are indexed [tier0, tier1, tier2, tier3] = white/aqua/purple/rainbow effect text.
sourceNote: Per-tier numbers from the guide Beacon List tab. It is authoritative on magnitudes but PARTIALLY STALE on rules (see aqua conflict) — same tab-drift pattern as the Missions tab.
